content.text:
| MCP:QnbszB | # Fastapi Project Template
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | A FastAPI project template using Make, Docker Compose and Github Actions,
| MCP:QnbszB | that includes **SQLAlchemy**, **Alembic** and an integration test setup.
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | [![Code Integration](https://github.com/bitestreams/fastapi-template/actions/workflows/code-integration.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/bitestreams/fastapi-template/actions/workflows/code-integration.yml)
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | ##  Installation
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | The only requirements to develop are **Docker** and **Make**.
| MCP:QnbszB | Docker makes it easy to get started and enables easier switching between projects, operating systems and machines.
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | To start up the project locally you first clone the project, and then run the following command in the cloned directory:
| MCP:QnbszB | ```sh
| MCP:QnbszB | $ git clone https://github.com/Donnype/fastapi-template.git
| MCP:QnbszB | $ cd fastapi-template
| MCP:QnbszB | $ make up
| MCP:QnbszB | ```
| MCP:QnbszB | Then to update the schema using alembic, in another shell:
| MCP:QnbszB | ```sh
| MCP:QnbszB | $ make migrate
| MCP:QnbszB | ```
| MCP:QnbszB | **That's it**. The app is set up and should be running at [localhost:5000/docs](localhost:5000/docs).
| MCP:QnbszB | Code changes are automatically detected using a mounted docker volume.
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | ### Makefile
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | ---
| MCP:QnbszB | The Makefile is the 'entrypoint' for the tools in this structure,
| MCP:QnbszB | such that you can easily run different commands without remembering the exact arguments.
| MCP:QnbszB | Run `make help` to get an overview of the available commands:
| MCP:QnbszB | ```sh
| MCP:QnbszB | $ make help
| MCP:QnbszB | up:
| MCP:QnbszB |  Run the application
| MCP:QnbszB | done: check test
| MCP:QnbszB |  Prepare for a commit
| MCP:QnbszB | test: utest itest
| MCP:QnbszB |  Run unit and integration tests
| MCP:QnbszB | utest: cleantest
| MCP:QnbszB |  Run unit tests
| MCP:QnbszB | itest: cleantest
| MCP:QnbszB |  Run integration tests
| MCP:QnbszB | check: cleantest
| MCP:QnbszB |  Check the code base
| MCP:QnbszB | cleantest:
| MCP:QnbszB |  Clean up test containers
| MCP:QnbszB | migrations:
| MCP:QnbszB |  Generate a migration using alembic
| MCP:QnbszB | migrate:
| MCP:QnbszB |  Run migrations upgrade using alembic
| MCP:QnbszB | downgrade:
| MCP:QnbszB |  Run migrations downgrade using alembic
| MCP:QnbszB | help:
| MCP:QnbszB |  Display this help message
| MCP:QnbszB | ```
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | Make allows you to collect common scripts and commands for the project.
| MCP:QnbszB | Other projects using different programming languages and frameworks get the same development interface by using Make.
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | ---
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | ### Installation without Docker
| MCP:QnbszB | To install a local copy of the python environment (to get code-completion for example),
| MCP:QnbszB | you must have **Poetry** installed.
| MCP:QnbszB | Create a poetry environment by running `poetry install` at the root of the project.
| MCP:QnbszB | To start up the server in the poetry environment and talk to a test sqlite database in `./test.db`, run
| MCP:QnbszB | ```bash
| MCP:QnbszB | $ export DB_STRING=sqlite:///test.db/
| MCP:QnbszB | $ poetry run alembic upgrade head && poetry run uvicorn api.main:app --port 5000 --reload
| MCP:QnbszB | ```
| MCP:QnbszB | Again, the app should be all set up and running at [localhost:5000/docs](localhost:5000/docs).
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | ### Tests
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | ---
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | The tests are containerized and the Docker setup can be found in the `.ci/` folder.
| MCP:QnbszB | They are written using Pytest.
| MCP:QnbszB | You can run the tests using:
| MCP:QnbszB | ```bash
| MCP:QnbszB | $ make test
| MCP:QnbszB | ```
| MCP:QnbszB | This runs the integration & unit tests.
| MCP:QnbszB | If you want to run them separately, use `make itest` to run the integration tests and `make utest` to run the unit tests.
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | ## Further reading
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | To read about the benefits of using this template,
| MCP:QnbszB | check out [my blog post](https://bitestreams.com/blog/fastapi_template/).
| MCP:QnbszB | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: c2b235985b32d335f0ccbd5a6fc7d1c40d867c01)

| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | [alembic]
| MCP:QnbszB | script_location = api/migrations
| MCP:QnbszB | prepend_sys_path = .
| MCP:QnbszB | version_path_separator = os  # Use os.pathsep. Default configuration used for new projects.
| MCP:QnbszB | sqlalchemy.url = driver://user:pass@localhost/dbname
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | [post_write_hooks]
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | [loggers]
| MCP:QnbszB | keys = root,sqlalchemy,alembic
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | [handlers]
| MCP:QnbszB | keys = console
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | [formatters]
| MCP:QnbszB | keys = generic
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | [logger_root]
| MCP:QnbszB | level = WARN
| MCP:QnbszB | handlers = console
| MCP:QnbszB | qualname =
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | [logger_sqlalchemy]
| MCP:QnbszB | level = WARN
| MCP:QnbszB | handlers =
| MCP:QnbszB | qualname = sqlalchemy.engine
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | [logger_alembic]
| MCP:QnbszB | level = INFO
| MCP:QnbszB | handlers =
| MCP:QnbszB | qualname = alembic
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | [handler_console]
| MCP:QnbszB | class = StreamHandler
| MCP:QnbszB | args = (sys.stderr,)
| MCP:QnbszB | level = NOTSET
| MCP:QnbszB | formatter = generic
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | [formatter_generic]
| MCP:QnbszB | format = %(levelname)-5.5s [%(name)s] %(message)s
| MCP:QnbszB | datefmt = %H:%M:%S
| MCP:QnbszB | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: d202c38a9dd8172e76072e244ad5292d61b67435)

| MCP:QnbszB | SHELL := bash
| MCP:QnbszB | .ONESHELL:
| MCP:QnbszB | .SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1
| MCP:QnbszB | export COMPOSE_DOCKER_CLI_BUILD=1
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | targets: help
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | up: ## Run the application
| MCP:QnbszB | 	docker compose up -d --build api
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | done: check test ## Prepare for a commit
| MCP:QnbszB | test: utest itest  ## Run unit and integration tests
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | ci-compose := docker compose -f .ci/docker-compose.yml
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | utest: cleantest ## Run unit tests
| MCP:QnbszB | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm unit pytest -m unit .
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | itest: cleantest ## Run integration tests
| MCP:QnbszB | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm integration pytest -m integration .
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | check: cleantest ## Check the code base
| MCP:QnbszB | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm unit pre-commit run -a
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | cleantest:  ## Clean up test containers
| MCP:QnbszB | 	$(ci-compose) kill
| MCP:QnbszB | 	$(ci-compose) down --remove-orphans
| MCP:QnbszB | 	$(ci-compose) build
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | ## Migrations
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | migrations: ## Generate a migration using alembic
| MCP:QnbszB | ifeq ($(m),)
| MCP:QnbszB | 	@echo "Specify a message with m={message} and a rev-id with revid={revid} (e.g. 0001 etc.)"; exit 1
| MCP:QnbszB | else ifeq ($(revid),)
| MCP:QnbszB | 	@echo "Specify a message with m={message} and a rev-id with revid={revid} (e.g. 0001 etc.)"; exit 1
| MCP:QnbszB | else
| MCP:QnbszB | 	docker compose run api alembic revision --autogenerate -m "$(m)" --rev-id="$(revid)"
| MCP:QnbszB | endif
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | migrate: ## Run migrations upgrade using alembic
| MCP:QnbszB | 	docker compose run --rm api alembic upgrade head
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | downgrade: ## Run migrations downgrade using alembic
| MCP:QnbszB | 	docker compose run --rm api alembic downgrade -1
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | help: ## Display this help message
| MCP:QnbszB | 	@awk -F '##' '/^[a-z_]+:[a-z ]+##/ { print "\033[34m"$$1"\033[0m" "\n" $$2 }' Makefile

| MCP:QnbszB | version: "3.7"
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | services:
| MCP:QnbszB |   api:
| MCP:QnbszB |     depends_on:
| MCP:QnbszB |       - db
| MCP:QnbszB |     ports:
| MCP:QnbszB |       - "127.0.0.1:5000:5000"
| MCP:QnbszB |     build:
| MCP:QnbszB |       dockerfile: ./api/Dockerfile
| MCP:QnbszB |       context: .
| MCP:QnbszB |     volumes:
| MCP:QnbszB |       - ./api:/home/user/app/api
| MCP:QnbszB |     environment:
| MCP:QnbszB |       - DB_STRING=postgresql://postgres:test@db:5432/postgres
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB |   db:
| MCP:QnbszB |     image: postgres:14.5
| MCP:QnbszB |     ports:
| MCP:QnbszB |       - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432"
| MCP:QnbszB |     volumes:
| MCP:QnbszB |       - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
| MCP:QnbszB |     environment:
| MCP:QnbszB |       - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test
| MCP:QnbszB | 
| MCP:QnbszB | volumes:
| MCP:QnbszB |   postgres-data:

data.content:
Here's a summary of what I found:

---

## Documented Non-Docker Setup Path

The README's **"Installation without Docker"** section outlines this workflow:

1. **Prerequisite** – Install [Poetry](https://python-poetry.org/) on your local machine.
2. **Create the Python environment** – From the project root, run:
   ```sh
   poetry install
   ```
3. **Set the database connection string** – Export the environment variable `DB_STRING`. The README demonstrates using a local SQLite database:
   ```sh
   export DB_STRING=sqlite:///test.db/
   ```
4. **Run migrations and start the server**:
   ```sh
   poetry run alembic upgrade head && poetry run uvicorn api.main:app --port 5000 --reload
   ```

The app is then available at `localhost:5000/docs`.

---

## Database Connection String Variable

The environment variable that **must** be set is:

> **`DB_STRING`**

This is used consistently across both paths:
- **Non-Docker** (README): `export DB_STRING=sqlite:///test.db/` (SQLite)
- **Docker** (`docker-compose.yml`): `DB_STRING=postgresql://postgres:test@db:5432/postgres` (PostgreSQL)
